Ad Text:
About the Opportunity

Work collaboratively with talented colleagues in a boutique future-focused CBD consultancy. Grow long-term client partnerships by delivering the leadership and expertise they seek to navigate an increasingly demanding and fast-paced digital world.

Your contribution will be as our technical authority on data architecture. Particularly Microsoft. You'll set direction, define standards, support sales and marketing, attend/host meetups/events and mentor colleagues. Plus, plenty of hands-on architecture across a diverse client base spanning health, government, mining and infrastructure.

This is an important position for us and a chance for you to build something special as we further grow our data practice toward an AI empowered future and the data needed to serve it.




About Interfuze

We're a Perth CBD consultancy that's deliberately high-end and sized to that Goldilocks zone of small enough to care, big enough to do the job. To us small means collaboration, accountability, quality and agility. The tight culture needed to tackle tomorrow's challenges.

We are a Microsoft Solution Partner, an Atlassian Gold Partner, and working hard toward becoming a key AI partner. Our clients often trust us with complex, consequential challenges – work that will only grow in the coming years.

We're determinedly professional and quietly confident, but grounded in a pragmatic, egoless culture that makes us genuinely approachable. It's a culture we've built carefully over our seven years — tracked through fifty client and employee surveys and kept alive every Thursday when we all come together for our “all in” days.




What we're looking for

Above all we are looking for leadership, ability, application and maturity. If you have these and most of the below, we recommend you apply.

8+ years technical experience, with 5+ years in data and solution architecture

Deep expertise in Microsoft data platforms — Azure Data Factory, Purview, Fabric, Power BI…

Enterprise level capability: e.g. DataOps, DevOps & IaC

Determinedly professional and filled with ability, balanced against a pragmatic and approachable ethos

Eager to apply your skills in the service of others

Clear communicator — comfortable with both engineers and executives

Experience mentoring colleagues and client teams

Perth resident with independent Australian working rights

Prior consulting experience preferred

Strong professional networks




What we offer

Competitive salary

Future-focused strategy

Flexible hybrid working arrangements

Cross-pollination with experienced consultants across multiple domains

Access to training, certifications, and conferences

Seasoned directors with domain understanding and an open-door policy

Minimum monthly 1:1s

Regular technical workshops and staff events (check our LinkedIn)

Thursday "All ins" filled with energy, foosball comps and food platters (healthy options of course)

Work-life integration to support you thrive both professionally and personally







The Details




Role Overview

Required

Design and deliver data architectures across analytics, integration, and governance on Azure and Microsoft platforms

Set technical direction — track and evaluate tech and trends, define patterns, standards, and reference architectures others build from

Mentor technical professionals and actively develop their data architecture capability

Guide clients on data strategy, modernisation, and cloud migration

Contribute to proposals and tenders — you'll be part of how we win good work

Champion data governance, security, and responsible data practices across all engagements

Nice to have

Active in Microsoft networks or community

Data visualisation and storytelling skills

Exposure to data science and ML platforms

Experience in the health sector

Familiarity with Atlassian data and reporting capabilities




Detailed technical skills breakdown

Advanced skills expected across most areas below, with willingness to learn and develop in others.

Solution architecture

Proven experience designing complex, multi-component data systems

Architectural decision-making with strategic impact

Strong understanding of Azure cloud data platforms

Experience with architectural governance

Data modelling & design

Deep expertise in data modelling approaches (relational and NoSQL)

Modern data architecture patterns — lakehouse, medallion, etc.

Design for scale, performance, and maintainability

Data strategy

Developing data strategies aligned with business goals

Data maturity models and assessment approaches

Experience with modernisation and transformation programs

Business intelligence

BI and analytics solution design

Power BI architecture and development

Balancing enterprise and self-service analytics needs

Technology & operations

DevOps, DataOps, and operational practices

ETL/ELT design patterns and orchestration

API design for data services

Technology governance and standards definition

Information security & governance

Data security and privacy principles

Compliance requirements (Privacy Act, GDPR)

Designing secure data architectures

Job Title: ARC Researcher (A) – AI Agents & Reinforcement Learning
Ad Text:
Based in School of Computer and Information Technology (Mawson Lakes)
Full-time (1.0 FTE), 2-year fixed term with a possibility for +1-year extension
Salary Range: $102,024 - $109,307 per annum (plus 17% superannuation)

At Adelaide University, we create the opportunities you need to achieve your ambitions – because when you thrive, we thrive. 

We are transforming education for contemporary learners and global citizens. Building on a proud legacy and shaped by bold ambition, it’s a place of excellence and equity, where our vibrant community of staff are united by our purpose to inspire Australia’s future change-makers and create a better tomorrow. 

Work that matters

Advance the frontier of AI by developing multi-agent systems capable of reasoning, planning, and autonomous execution.
The role requires the ability to produce high-quality peer-reviewed outputs, develop and evaluate innovative AI methods, and contribute to the supervision of research students. Strong communication and collaboration skills are essential, as is the ability to work effectively both independently and within multidisciplinary teams, engaging with academic and industry stakeholders.
This role will deliver impactful research outcomes and contribute to real-world applications, including virtual satellite agent systems.

Curious to learn more? Explore the full role description to discover more about this opportunity.

The team

Reporting to the Principal Investigator, this role is part of a leading AI research group specialising in reinforcement learning and intelligent systems. The team is focused on producing world-class research while collaborating with industry to translate innovation into practical solutions.

Visit the AU website to learn more about the School of Computer and Information Technology.

Our people

Our people are guided by purpose, curiosity and a commitment to lifelong learning. We embrace authenticity, innovation and collaboration, and harness diverse thinking in our pursuit of excellence.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a dynamic, research-intensive environment. You will collaborate with academic and industry partners, contributing to innovative projects that bridge cutting-edge theory with real-world deployment.

Learn more about our people, what we stand for and what we offer at Careers at AU.

Experience

To join our community and thrive in this role, you will likely have the following skills and experience:

Qualifications

PhD in Computer Science, AI, Machine Learning or related field

Experience

Strong track record of publications in top-tier venues (e.g. CORE A*)
Expertise in reinforcement learning, AI agents, and LLM reasoning
Advanced programming and experimental research skills
Experience developing, implementing, and evaluating AI methods
Capability to contribute to supervision of research students

Our commitment to inclusion and diversity

We are committed to fostering a culture of inclusion where diversity is celebrated and everyone feels respected and valued. Adelaide University is an equal opportunity employer, committed to creating a safe, inclusive, and equitable workplace where everyone can thrive. We strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disability, and people of all ages, genders, cultural backgrounds, sexual orientations, and gender identities. We are committed to supporting flexible working arrangements and providing reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process.

Launch your future with Adelaide University now

Applying is simple. Simply click on the Apply Now button and upload:

your current CV
a cover letter that tells us why you’re excited about the role

The online application form will list the specific selection criteria that you need to address.

Submit your application by 11.30pm 20 April 2026

The University reserves the right to close this advertisement before the closing date if a suitable candidate is identified. Please note that the role description is not attached to this advertisement as it is currently being finalised. 

For further information about this opportunity, please contact (quoting reference number 492761):

Simone Patterson
Talent Acquisition Adviser
+61 8 8302 1700| careers.adelaideuniversity@adelaide.edu.au

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little different. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions. 

With millions of customer interactions across our ecosystem and a uniquely rich dataset spanning customer behaviour, travel and partner engagement, data plays a central role in how we personalise experiences and make decisions at scale.

 

About the role:

We’re looking for a Senior Data Scientist to join our Advanced Analytics Centre of Excellence. In this role, you’ll build, deploy and scale machine learning and experimentation solutions that directly shape loyalty strategy, customer decisioning and personalisation, partner performance, and customer experience across the Velocity ecosystem.

You’ll work in a cross functional environment alongside Insight Analysts, Analytics Engineers and business stakeholders, leading complex problem spaces end to end from framing and experiment design through to production deployment and measurable business impact, embedding models into production systems that power large scale customer and member decisioning.

You’ll report to the Manager, Personalisation Analytics and Data Science and join a team with established models in production, including recommender systems, propensity and regression models, supported by an evolving MLOps framework built on Databricks and AutoML. These models are actively used to power large scale customer and member decisioning across the Velocity program.

You’ll play a key role in shaping and owning the data science roadmap, working closely with the manager to prioritise initiatives and ensure delivery against commitments.

As Velocity accelerates its AI transformation journey, you’ll partner closely with our AI teams to identify, develop and embed AI driven capabilities into scalable products and decisioning frameworks.

 

In this role you will:

Drive the data science roadmap for your domain, identifying and prioritising high impact opportunities aligned to business objectives and ensuring delivery against agreed commitments
Design, build and deploy statistical and machine learning models using Python and Spark within a modern, scalable data and ML platform, including Databricks, with a focus on deploying models into production environments supporting batch and increasingly near real time decisioning
Lead the end to end lifecycle of models, from problem framing and feature engineering through to deployment, monitoring and optimisation
Develop and enhance recommender systems, propensity models and predictive models that drive commercial outcomes
Define and evolve experimentation frameworks (A/B and beyond) to inform decision making, measure causal impact and continuously optimise outcomes
Own the evolution of MLOps practices within data science, driving improvements in model deployment, monitoring and lifecycle management in partnership with engineering teams
Build scalable data products and reusable decisioning frameworks
Provide technical leadership and mentorship, raising the bar for data science across the team
Translate complex analyses into clear, actionable insights using effective data visualisation and storytelling for both technical and non technical audiences
Influence senior stakeholders and partner closely with analytics, engineering and business teams to embed data-driven decision making into workflows
Ensure all work aligns with Virgin Australia Group’s safety, security and resilience standards

 

You’ll be great in this role if you have:

A degree in a quantitative discipline such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics or Computer Science (PhD desirable)
Typically 5+ years of experience building and deploying machine learning models in a commercial environment
Proven experience leading end to end data science initiatives with measurable business impact
Proficiency in Python, with strong SQL skills and experience working with large and complex datasets (Spark desirable)
Experience working with modern data and ML platforms such as Databricks and/or AWS
A strong foundation in statistics, experimental design and causal inference
Experience shaping or working within MLOps frameworks and production ML environments
Strong communication and data visualisation skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive data-driven decisions with measurable business impact
Demonstrated ability to operate in ambiguous environments and translate business problems into scalable data science solutions
Curiosity, ownership and a drive to automate and scale solutions
Experience in loyalty, travel or consumer products (nice to have)

 

Why this role is interesting:

Work on production grade machine learning systems that power customer decisioning at scale
Build on an existing ML and MLOps ecosystem with modern tooling (Databricks, AutoML)
Own and shape the data science roadmap and drive delivery of high impact initiatives
Help scale experimentation, personalisation and AI driven decisioning across Velocity

Candidates must be Sydney based, or willing to relocate.

 

What you’ll get from us 

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to): 

Discounted flights for you and your loved ones (plus $1000 travel credits per year) 
Flexible and hybrid working, with a mix of working from home and the office 
‘Dress for Your Day’ – wear whatever makes you feel great for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you 
Discounts on travel insurance, lounge membership, car hire, accommodation, and experiences worldwide 
Cheaper hospitality, retail, technology, beauty and wellness services 
Free counselling and online tools to support your physical, mental, social and financial health. 

 

When everyone is included, everyone wins 

We’re all individuals. And we love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTIQ+ community, and anyone else who wants to join us. 

 

Ready to apply? 

Working at Virgin Australia isn't just a job. It's a passion that becomes part of you. Are you ready to join us? Get ready for a rewarding experience. Apply today!

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer - Snowflake - Fully Remote / WFH
Salary: Up to $1100 pd Inc Super, 100% Remote/WFH
Ad Text:
Day Rate up to $1100 pd inc Super
100% WFH/ Remote, min 6+ month contract
Federal Government end client 
 
Company Overview

Our client is a technology‑led organisation delivering enterprise‑scale data and analytics solutions across for a large Federal Government end client. They work with modern data platforms and cloud‑native architectures, enabling their teams to solve meaningful problems at scale while maintaining high engineering standards.

The business is known for investing in its people, technical excellence, and delivering outcomes that matter.
  
Role Overview

This Senior Data Engineer will play a key role in designing, building and deploying modern data platforms primarily on Snowflake. Reporting into a Data Engineering Lead, you will partner closely with analytics, AI/ML, platform and business stakeholders to deliver robust, production‑ready data solutions.

Your focus will be on developing scalable, high‑performance data pipelines and architectures that support analytics, reporting and AI‑driven applications across batch and real‑time use cases.

Core responsibilities include

Design and deploy modern data architectures using Medallion and Lakehouse patterns on Snowflake
Build complex, code‑based ETL/ELT pipelines using Snowflake native features, Snowpipe and dbt
Develop performance‑optimised data models leveraging Snowflake capabilities
Work with Big Data, including PII handling, data wrangling, formatting and preparation
Apply engineering best practices across coding standards, version control, CI/CD, monitoring and alerting
Implement infrastructure‑as‑code and cloud‑native patterns across AWS, Azure or GCP
Productionise AI models by building and monitoring pipelines that feed batch and real‑time inferencing applications
Collaborate closely with cross‑functional teams to deliver enterprise‑grade data solutions
  
Skills & Experience

Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for data engineering use cases
Proven experience building and deploying data engineering pipelines into production environments
Hands‑on experience with the Snowflake ecosystem, including features such as Workflows, Dynamic Tables and Snowpipe
Solid experience working with modern cloud platforms including AWS (preferred), Azure or GCP
Practical experience implementing CI/CD, automation and cloud architecture best practices
Experience delivering medium to large‑scale data engineering projects in enterprise environments
Aust Citizen required
  
What’s on Offer

People enjoy working in this team because they are trusted to solve real problems, work with modern technologies and contribute meaningfully to technical direction.

What you can expect

Great daily rate and fully remote /flexible working arrangements
Exposure to large‑scale, modern data platforms and cloud architectures
Opportunity to work on AI‑enabled and real‑time data use cases
Strong focus on engineering quality, learning and professional growth
Supportive, collaborative and inclusive culture
  
Click on the APPLY button or contact Dave on David.Marshall@profusingroup.com for a confidential discussion.
  
We are committed to providing a supportive culture and positively contributing towards creating diverse and inclusive workplaces for our candidates and clients. We invite candidates of all ages, genders, sexual orientation, cultural backgrounds, people with disability, neurodiverse individuals, and Indigenous Australians to apply.

6 months contract
Hybrid (Melbourne Based)
Citizen with atleast Baseline Clearance

5+ years of experience in MuleSoft/Tibco Integration Specialist.

contact Shilpa Chopra at shilpac@greenlightworldwide.com or call at +61482072637 for a confidential discussion.

Company description:



Accelerate AI adoption. Influence how we hire.

Established in 1840, we're Australia's first member-owned wellbeing company. Today we have over $1 billion in revenue and provide smart solutions and services to more than 700,000 Australians. We employ over 7000 people and our purpose is to Help People to Thrive.

It's an exciting time to be joining Australian Unity - we have grown significantly over recent years and are transforming to capitalise on further growth opportunities to help our customers and employees thrive. We operate with commercial principles and with a strong social purpose to create community value. Australian Unity is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.



Job description:



You will lead the end‑to‑end adoption of AI solutions within our People & Culture function - from identifying opportunities to delivering real business value. A major early focus will be partnering with our recruitment platform vendor to ensure their AI models meet our standards for fairness, transparency, performance, and compliance.

In this role, you will:

Lead end‑to‑end AI adoption within a business squad - from identifying opportunities and shaping requirements to solution selection, delivery, and post‑launch support.
Work directly with AI vendors - challenging assumptions, asking the right technical questions, evaluating model behaviour, ensuring fairness and performance, and influencing product direction.
Partner with internal technical and business stakeholders to shape use cases, drive adoption, build user confidence, and ensure AI solutions deliver measurable business value.
Ensure all AI initiatives meet governance, risk, and compliance expectations, and that solutions are well‑designed, documented, and aligned with enterprise standards.
Contribute to the evolution of enterprise‑level AI practices in an area such as AI literacy & change, AI governance, or AIOps / AI innovation.

You'll be part of a small, expert group helping Australian Unity become an AI‑native enterprise - and you'll have the freedom to shape how we get there.

About You:

We're looking for someone with a strong technical foundation who is ready to apply it in a more product‑oriented role. You may come from a software engineering or computer science background with hands‑on AI experience, and you've either already made the move into product-style work - or you're excited to take that next step.

You enjoy solving business problems, engaging with stakeholders, and challenging vendors with the right technical questions. You're as comfortable discussing model behaviour and risks as you are explaining concepts to non‑technical audiences.

To be successful in the role you will be able to demonstrate the following capabilities / experience:

Hands‑on industry experience (3+ years) in software engineering, AI engineering, data science, or computer science
Practical experience applying AI/ML/LLM technologies in real-world projects
Experience translating business needs into technical or product requirements
Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify complex AI concepts for non‑technical audiences
Experience delivering solutions in regulated or risk-aware environments (privacy, fairness, model behaviour) is a plus
Exposure to AI governance, AI literacy/change, AIOps/MLOps/LLMOps, model evaluation, or data privacy considerations (desirable)
Hands-on experience with low-code and automation platforms e.g., Microsoft Power Automate, Copilot Studio (desirable)

What's on Offer:

You will enjoy a range of great employee benefits and rewards including:

Competitive salary + bonus program
Enjoy additional yearly Well-Being and Community leave days
14-week paid parental leave, with equal benefit for both parents
Employee Referral Program
Employee Assistance Program - support and assistance for you and your partner at those times in life when you need it most
Maxxia Rewards - a great range of discounts and benefits at selected retail outlets, department stores, attractions, travel, cinemas, restaurants.
Discounts across the Australian Unity business including Private Health Insurance, General Insurance + more!
Available access to LinkedIn Learning courses through our great Learning platform

For further benefits visit: https://www.australianunity.com.au/careers/culture

Australian Unity is an Equal Opportunity employer and we encourage applications from all members of the community, including people of Aboriginal and Torres strait Islander descent, culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds and, mature aged people.

Want to build the AI Infrastructure that changes an entire Industry?

Ready to step into the principal seat and architect something from the ground up?




🚀 About Pivot Wealth

Pivot Wealth is one of Australia’s fastest-growing and most awarded financial advice firms. We work with high performing professionals and business owners to help them create a life not limited by money. 

We believe the future of advice will be built at the intersection of human expertise and intelligent technology. While thousands of advisers have left the industry in recent years, client demand continues to rise. We see this as an opportunity to build scalable, high quality infrastructure that allows great advice to reach more Australians. 

We are commercially ambitious, technically progressive and deeply focused on outcomes.




🔒 About the Opportunity

We’re hiring a hands on Principal Engineer to own architecture, AI capability and engineering standards inside a fast growing financial services firm.

You’ll operate as the most senior technical authority in the business, working closely with leadership to translate commercial priorities into scalable, secure engineering outcomes.

There is no CTO above you. No VP Engineering. No technical hierarchy.

This is a hands on hybrid role. You will architect, review and write code, but your primary value is setting direction, raising engineering standards and leading the engineering team.

This role is based in Sydney, with flexibility, but you must be comfortable operating as the sole senior technical authority.




🎯 What You’ll Own

As the Principal Engineer, you will:

Own platform architecture across cloud, applications, data and integrations

Set and enforce engineering standards including code review, QA and release discipline

Establish production grade environments with proper dev, staging and deployment controls

Manage the AI and data platform from ingestion through to warehouse and vector stores

Ship AI enabled products into production with guardrails, evaluation and observability

Design automation that reduces operational workload and improves throughput

Direct offshore engineering execution and act as senior technical reviewer

Ensure security, data sovereignty and regulatory alignment by design

You are not:

An IT helpdesk

A passive senior developer waiting for tasks

A diagram drawing architect who delegates execution




🧠 Technical Environment

You must be comfortable operating deeply within the following stack:

GCP native, AU sovereign. Cloud Run, Cloud SQL (PostgreSQL), BigQuery, Firestore. GitHub CI/CD.

AI layer includes Vertex AI, Document AI with custom trained models, vector stores, and deterministic + LLM hybrid architectures.

TypeScript, Python, SQL, Shell, Rust. Complex SaaS integration ecosystem.

Depth in GCP is strongly preferred.




🎟️ Your Ticket to the Game

We are looking for someone who has:

5 to 8 years of Engineering experience, and ready to step into a principal-level role

Proven ownership of end-to-end architecture for production systems

Proven experience delivering AI-enabled digital solutions into production

Experience with vector stores and RAG architectures

Experience implementing AI guardrails, observability, and human-in-the-loop validation

Experience leading and directing engineering teams

Ability to create and drive a roadmap without waiting for instructions

Exceptional communication skills - can communicate technical information to a non-technical audience


🏆 Why Work With Us?

You will lead the technical direction of the entire company

You will build AI infrastructure that materially changes how advice is delivered

Be part of an AFR Fast 100, high-growth environment

Market leading package including super, bonus and ESOP

Direct access to our founder and leadership team, no politics or bureaucracy 




Ready?

This role is for someone ready to step into full technical ownership.

If you’ve built, shipped and led production systems and you’re ready to architect at company level, apply with your CV and cover letter outlining the most significant AI solution you’ve personally owned end-to-end.
